children.heading: Allocation to processors
children.label: a Allocation to processors
children.text: Whenever marketing allotments are established for a crop year under , in order to afford all interested persons an equitable opportunity to market sugar under an allotment, the Secretary shall allocate each such allotment among the processors covered by the allotment.section 1359cc of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall make allocations for cane sugar after a hearing, if requested by the affected sugarcane processors and growers, and on such notice as the Secretary by regulation may prescribe, in such manner and in such quantities as to provide a fair, efficient, and equitable distribution of the allocations under this paragraph. Each such allocation shall be subject to adjustment under .section 1359cc(g) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: past marketings of sugar, based on the average of the 2 highest years of production of raw cane sugar from among the 1996 through 2000 crops;

children.label: ii
children.text: the ability of processors to market sugar covered by that portion of the allotment allocated for the crop year; and

children.label: iii
children.text: past processings of sugar from sugarcane, based on the average of the 3 highest years of production during the 1996 through 2000 crop years.

children.heading: Talisman processing facility
children.label: C Talisman processing facility
children.text: In the case of allotments under subparagraph (B) attributable to the operations of the Talisman processing facility before , the Secretary shall allocate the allotment among processors in the State under subparagraph (A) in accordance with the agreements of March 25 and 26, 1999, between the affected processors and the Secretary of the Interior.May 13, 2002

children.label: i
children.text: past marketings of sugar, based on the average of the 2 highest years of production of raw cane sugar from among the 1997 through 2001 crop years;

children.label: ii
children.text: the ability of processors to market sugar covered by that portion of the allotments allocated for the crop year; and

children.label: iii
children.text: past processings of sugar from sugarcane, based on the average of the 2 highest crop years of crop production during the 1997 through 2001 crop years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraphs (B) and (D), the Secretary, on application of any processor that begins processing sugarcane on or after , and after a hearing (if requested by the affected sugarcane processors and growers) and on such notice as the Secretary by regulation may prescribe, may provide the processor with an allocation that provides a fair, efficient and equitable distribution of the allocations from the allotment for the State in which the processor is located.May 13, 2002

children.heading: Proportionate share States
children.label: ii Proportionate share States
children.text: In the case of proportionate share States, the Secretary shall establish proportionate shares in a quantity sufficient to produce the sugarcane required to satisfy the allocations.

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of the first crop year of operation of a new processor, 50,000 short tons (raw value); and

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of each subsequent crop year of operation of the new processor, a quantity established by the Secretary in accordance with this subparagraph and the criteria described in subparagraph (B) or (D), as applicable.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraphs (A) and (C) of , to accommodate an allocation under clause (i) to a new processor located in a new entrant mainland State, the Secretary shall provide the new entrant mainland State with an allotment.section 1359cc(e)(3) of this title

children.heading: Effect on other allotments
children.label: II Effect on other allotments
children.text: The allotment to any new entrant mainland State shall be subtracted, on a pro rata basis, from the allotments otherwise allotted to each mainland State under .section 1359cc(e)(3) of this title

children.heading: Adverse effects
children.label: v Adverse effects
children.text: Before providing an initial processor allocation or State allotment to a new entrant processor or a new entrant State under this subparagraph, the Secretary shall take into consideration any adverse effects that the provision of the allocation or allotment may have on existing cane processors and producers in mainland States.

children.heading: Ability to market
children.label: vi Ability to market
children.text: Consistent with and this section, any processor allocation or State allotment made to a new entrant processor or to a new entrant State under this subparagraph shall be provided only after the applicant processor, or the applicable processors in the State, have demonstrated the ability to process, produce, and market (including the transfer or delivery of the raw cane sugar to a refinery for further processing or marketing) raw cane sugar for the crop year for which the allotment is applicable.section 1359cc of this title

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: vii Prohibition
children.text: Not more than 1 processor allocation provided under this subparagraph may be applicable to any individual sugar processing facility.

children.heading: Transfer of ownership
children.label: F Transfer of ownership
children.text: If a sugarcane processor is sold or otherwise transferred to another owner or is closed as part of an affiliated corporate group processing consolidation, the Secretary shall transfer the allotment allocation for the processor to the purchaser, new owner, successor in interest, or any remaining processor of an affiliated entity, as applicable, of the processor.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this paragraph and sections 1359cc(g), 1359ee(b), and 1359ff(b) of this title, the Secretary shall make allocations for beet sugar among beet sugar processors for each crop year that allotments are in effect on the basis of the adjusted weighted average quantity of beet sugar produced by the processors for each of the 1998 through 2000 crop years, as determined under this paragraph.

children.heading: Quantity
children.label: B Quantity
children.text: The quantity of an allocation made for a beet sugar processor for a crop year under subparagraph (A) shall bear the same ratio to the quantity of allocations made for all beet sugar processors for the crop year as the adjusted weighted average quantity of beet sugar produced by the processor (as determined under subparagraphs (C) and (D)) bears to the total of the adjusted weighted average quantities of beet sugar produced by all processors (as so determined).

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of the 1998 crop year, 25 percent of the quantity of beet sugar produced by the processor during the crop year;

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of the 1999 crop year, 35 percent of the quantity of beet sugar produced by the processor during the crop year; and

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of the 2000 crop year, 40 percent of the quantity of beet sugar produced by the processor (including any quantity of sugar received from the Commodity Credit Corporation) during the crop year.

children.label: I
children.text: during the 1996 through 2000 crop years, opened a sugar beet processing factory;

children.label: II
children.text: during the 1998 through 2000 crop years, closed a sugar beet processing factory;

children.label: III
children.text: during the 1998 through 2000 crop years, constructed a molasses desugarization facility; or

children.label: IV
children.text: during the 1998 through 2000 crop years, suffered substantial quality losses on sugar beets stored during any such crop year.

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of a processor that opened a sugar beet processing factory, increased by 1.25 percent of the total of the adjusted weighted average quantities of beet sugar produced by all processors during the 1998 through 2000 crop years (without consideration of any adjustment under this subparagraph) for each sugar beet processing factory that is opened by the processor;

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a processor that closed a sugar beet processing factory, decreased by 1.25 percent of the total of the adjusted weighted average quantities of beet sugar produced by all processors during the 1998 through 2000 crop years (without consideration of any adjustment under this subparagraph) for each sugar beet processing factory that is closed by the processor;

children.label: III
children.text: in the case of a processor that constructed a molasses desugarization facility, increased by 0.25 percent of the total of the adjusted weighted average quantities of beet sugar produced by all processors during the 1998 through 2000 crop years (without consideration of any adjustment under this subparagraph) for each molasses desugarization facility that is constructed by the processor; and

children.label: IV
children.text: in the case of a processor that suffered substantial quality losses on stored sugar beets, increased by 1.25 percent of the total of the adjusted weighted average quantities of beet sugar produced by all processors during the 1998 through 2000 crop years (without consideration of any adjustment under this subparagraph).

children.heading: Permanent termination of operations of a processor
children.label: E Permanent termination of operations of a processor
i
children.text: eliminate the allocation of the processor provided under this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: distribute the allocation to other beet sugar processors on a pro rata basis.

children.heading: Sale of all assets of a processor to another processor
children.label: F Sale of all assets of a processor to another processor
children.text: If a processor of beet sugar (or all of the assets of the processor) is sold to another processor of beet sugar, the Secretary shall transfer the allocation of the seller to the buyer unless the allocation has been distributed to other sugar beet processors under subparagraph (E).

children.heading: Sale of factories of a processor to another processor
children.label: G Sale of factories of a processor to another processor

children.heading: Effect of sale
children.label: i Effect of sale
children.text: Subject to subparagraphs (E) and (F), if 1 or more factories of a processor of beet sugar (but not all of the assets of the processor) are sold to another processor of beet sugar during a crop year, the Secretary shall assign a pro rata portion of the allocation of the seller to the allocation of the buyer to reflect the historical contribution of the production of the sold 1 or more factories to the total allocation of the seller, unless the buyer and the seller have agreed upon the transfer of a different portion of the allocation of the seller, in which case, the Secretary shall transfer that portion agreed upon by the buyer and seller.

children.label: I
children.text: during the remainder of the crop year for which the sale described in clause (i) occurs; and

children.label: II
children.text: during each subsequent crop year.

children.heading: Use of other factories to fill allocation
children.label: iii Use of other factories to fill allocation
children.text: If the assignment of the allocation under clause (i) to the buyer for the 1 or more purchased factories cannot be filled by the production of the 1 or more purchased factories, the remainder of the allocation may be filled by beet sugar produced by the buyer from other factories of the buyer.

children.heading: New entrants starting production, reopening, or acquiring an existing factory with production history
children.label: H New entrants starting production, reopening, or acquiring an existing factory with production history
aa
children.text: does not have an allocation of the beet sugar allotment under this subpart;

children.label: bb
children.text: is not affiliated with any other individual, corporation, or entity that has an allocation of beet sugar under this subpart (referred to in this clause as a “third party”); and

children.label: cc
children.text: will process sugar beets produced by sugar beet growers under contract with the new entrant for the production of sugar at the new or re-opened factory that is the basis for the new entrant allocation.

children.label: aa
children.text: the third party has an ownership interest in the new entrant;

children.label: bb
children.text: the new entrant and the third party have owners in common;

children.label: cc
children.text: the third party has the ability to exercise control over the new entrant by organizational rights, contractual rights, or any other means;

children.label: dd
children.text: the third party has a contractual relationship with the new entrant by which the new entrant will make use of the facilities or assets of the third party; or

children.label: ee
children.text: there are any other similar circumstances by which the Secretary determines that the new entrant and the third party are affiliated.

children.heading: Allocation for a new entrant that has constructed a new factory or reopened a factory that was not operated since before 1998
children.label: ii Allocation for a new entrant that has constructed a new factory or reopened a factory that was not operated since before 1998
I
children.text: assign an allocation for beet sugar to the new entrant that provides a fair and equitable distribution of the allocations for beet sugar so as to enable the new entrant to achieve a factory utilization rate comparable to the factory utilization rates of other similarly-situated processors; and

children.label: II
children.text: reduce the allocations for beet sugar of all other processors on a pro rata basis to reflect the allocation to the new entrant.

children.heading: Allocation for a new entrant that has acquired an existing factory with a production history
children.label: iii Allocation for a new entrant that has acquired an existing factory with a production history

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: If a new entrant acquires an existing factory that has processed sugar beets from the 1998 or subsequent crop year and has a production history, on the mutual agreement of the new entrant and the company currently holding the allocation associated with the factory, the Secretary shall transfer to the new entrant a portion of the allocation of the current allocation holder to reflect the historical contribution of the production of the 1 or more sold factories to the total allocation of the current allocation holder, unless the new entrant and current allocation holder have agreed upon the transfer of a different portion of the allocation of the current allocation holder, in which case, the Secretary shall transfer that portion agreed upon by the new entrant and the current allocation holder.

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: II Prohibition
children.text: In the absence of a mutual agreement described in subclause (I), the new entrant shall be ineligible for a beet sugar allocation.

children.heading: Appeals
children.label: iv Appeals
children.text: Any decision made under this subsection may be appealed to the Secretary in accordance with .section 1359ii of this title
